title: perf(text-splitters): use deque for line consumption in markdown splitter
body_text:
## Problem

`ExperimentalMarkdownSyntaxTextSplitter.split_text()` and `_resolve_code_chunk()` use `list.pop(0)` to consume lines from the front of a list while parsing markdown.

`list.pop(0)` is **O(n)** because CPython must shift every remaining element left by one slot. On large markdown documents with thousands of lines, this accumulates to **O(n²)** total work.

## Solution

Convert `raw_lines` to a `collections.deque` at the start of `split_text()` and replace all `.pop(0)` calls with `.popleft()`, which is **O(1)** amortised.

`deque` supports the same boolean truthiness check and iteration used by both methods. The `_resolve_code_chunk` type annotation is updated to `deque[str]` to match.

## Testing

All 9 markdown-related tests in `tests/unit_tests/test_text_splitters.py` pass (2 consecutive clean runs).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

entities.name: ExperimentalMarkdownSyntaxTextSplitter
entities.description: A specific text splitter implementation designed for Markdown syntax.

entities.name: ExperimentalMarkdownSyntaxTextSplitter.split_text()
entities.description: A method within ExperimentalMarkdownSyntaxTextSplitter responsible for splitting text.

entities.name: _resolve_code_chunk()
entities.description: A helper method used in the context of Markdown parsing, likely within the text splitter.

entities.name: list.pop(0)
entities.description: A Python list method that removes and returns the element at index 0, which is inefficient for large lists.

entities.name: collections.deque
entities.description: A Python data structure (double-ended queue) optimized for efficient appends and pops from both ends.

entities.name: deque.popleft()
entities.description: A method of collections.deque for efficiently removing and returning an element from the left side.

entities.name: O(n)
entities.description: Linear time complexity, indicating that the execution time grows linearly with the input size.

entities.name: O(n^2)
entities.description: Quadratic time complexity, indicating a significant performance bottleneck where execution time grows quadratically with input size.

entities.name: O(1)
entities.description: Constant time complexity, indicating optimal efficiency where execution time remains constant regardless of input size.

entities.name: CPython
entities.description: The default and most common implementation of the Python programming language.

entities.name: Performance Issue
entities.description: A problem related to the speed or efficiency of a system's operation.

entities.name: Text Splitters
entities.description: A general component or module responsible for splitting text into smaller chunks.

entities.name: Copilot
entities.description: An AI assistant that co-authored the solution.

extracted_data.summary: The ExperimentalMarkdownSyntaxTextSplitter suffers from an O(n^2) performance issue due to inefficient list.pop(0) calls, which is resolved by switching to collections.deque and deque.popleft() for O(1) line consumption.
